title: Invoke CLASSIFY operations non-interactively
description: Provide deterministic structured process input, output, diagnostics, compatibility, actor, and idempotency behavior for every public CLASSIFY operation. Implements G5 and UC-CLASSIFY-006.
rationale: Agents must distinguish business outcomes from diagnostics and must never guess whether an evaluation, rule change, or Ledger mutation succeeded.
story_want: invoke classification through stable structured commands
story_so_that: automation handles success, failure, retry, and compatibility deterministically
acceptance_criteria: Given valid structured input for a public operation, when invocation completes, then exactly one versioned structured business result is written to stdout and the process exits with the documented category.

Given an expected validation, not-found, conflict, lifecycle, stale, compatibility, resource, or downstream LEDGER failure, when invocation completes, then one module-scoped machine-readable error is returned with the documented non-zero exit.
Given malformed input or a forbidden unknown field, when invocation is attempted, then validation fails before evaluation, rule-state change, or LEDGER mutation.
Given an unsupported CLASSIFY or required LEDGER contract version, when invocation is attempted, then compatibility fails before financial data read or mutation.
Given a mutating rule, feedback, abandon, cleanup, or apply operation, when its request lacks required actor context or idempotency identity, then CLASSIFY rejects it before mutation.
Given any successful or expected-failure invocation, when stderr is inspected, then it contains metadata-only diagnostics and no serialized request, result, or financial payload.
security_criteria: Process arguments, stdout errors, stderr, logs, crash diagnostics, and telemetry must not disclose private financial payloads.
